feed.title: Open Bankruptcy Project -- News & Analysis
feed.subtitle: Data-driven bankruptcy news and analysis from the Open Bankruptcy Project. Empirical research covering 4.9 million federal cases across all 94 judicial districts.

entry.title: What Happens to Your Bank Account in Bankruptcy?
entry.summary: Your bank accounts are not automatically frozen in bankruptcy, but the trustee can access non-exempt funds. Learn about exemptions, set-off risk, and practical tips.

entry.title: Does Bankruptcy Affect Your Spouse?
entry.summary: Filing bankruptcy does not automatically affect your spouse, but co-signed debts, joint accounts, and community property create important exceptions.

entry.title: How Much Debt Do You Need to File Bankruptcy?
entry.summary: There is no minimum debt requirement to file bankruptcy, but practical thresholds and the means test determine whether it makes financial sense.

entry.title: Bankruptcy vs Debt Settlement: Which Is Better?
entry.summary: A side-by-side comparison of bankruptcy and debt settlement covering cost, timeline, credit impact, and when each approach makes the most sense.

entry.title: Can I Keep My Tax Refund in Bankruptcy?
entry.summary: Whether you can keep your tax refund depends on the chapter, timing, and your state's exemption laws.

entry.title: What Debts Survive Bankruptcy?
entry.summary: Most debts are discharged in bankruptcy, but some survive. A plain-language guide to Section 523(a) and nondischargeable debts.

entry.title: How Long Does Chapter 7 Take?
entry.summary: Most Chapter 7 cases go from filing to discharge in about 3 to 4 months. Here is the timeline, milestone by milestone.

entry.title: What Is a Bankruptcy Trustee?
entry.summary: Bankruptcy trustees play different roles depending on the chapter. Learn what Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 trustees actually do.

entry.title: Can You File Bankruptcy More Than Once?
entry.summary: Yes, but waiting periods apply. Complete guide to 727(a)(8), 1328(f), and cross-chapter refiling timelines.

entry.title: What Happens When Chapter 13 Is Dismissed?
entry.summary: Dismissal ends bankruptcy protection and lets creditors resume collections. Learn the consequences and how Section 109(g) may affect refiling.

entry.title: Open Bankruptcy Project Launches Free Bankruptcy Resources Nationwide
entry.summary: The Open Bankruptcy Project has launched a network of free educational websites covering every major bankruptcy topic, with interactive tools, bilingual content in English and Spanish, and data from 4.9 million federal cases. No ads, no attorney referrals, no paywalls.

entry.title: 75,764 Estimated Section 1328(f) Violations: No Court Is Screening for Them
entry.summary: An estimated 75,764 Section 1328(f) timing violations have occurred nationally, and no federal court is systematically screening for them. District-by-district data from 4.9 million cases.

entry.title: Where Chapter 13 Fails: The 15 Worst Districts for Plan Completion (2020-2024)
entry.summary: National analysis of Chapter 13 plan completion rates reveals the 15 worst-performing federal districts from 2020-2024, with dismissal rates exceeding 60% in some courts.

entry.title: 1 in 3 Chapter 13 Filers Has Filed Before: Repeat Bankruptcy by District
entry.summary: District-by-district data on repeat bankruptcy filings shows approximately one-third of Chapter 13 filers have filed previously, raising questions about system effectiveness.

entry.title: Attorney Fee Transparency in Consumer Bankruptcy: A National Analysis
entry.summary: Fee disclosures in consumer bankruptcy happen after retention, lack standardization, and sit behind PACER's paywall. Open Bankruptcy Project data reveals significant concentration in the consumer bankruptcy bar.

entry.title: Bankruptcy Filings by State: 2026 National Overview
entry.summary: Comprehensive state-by-state breakdown of bankruptcy filing rates for 2026, with data on Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 distribution across all 50 states.

entry.title: Chapter 13 Completion Rates Vary Widely Across Federal Districts
entry.summary: National analysis shows Chapter 13 plan completion rates vary dramatically by federal district, ranging from under 20% to over 60%, driven by local legal culture and fee structures.

entry.title: The Means Test Gets Harder: April 2026 Median Income Increases
entry.summary: Updated median income figures for April 2026 raise the bar for the Chapter 7 means test, potentially pushing more filers toward Chapter 13.

entry.title: Pro Se Bankruptcy Filings: What the Data Shows
entry.summary: Data analysis of pro se (self-represented) bankruptcy filings across federal districts reveals patterns in filing rates, outcomes, and the gap between represented and unrepresented debtors.
